diagnosis:
The strategy detects OBV/price divergence over a configurable lookback window (default 20 bars). It first checks whether the precomputed `obv_divergence` feature (OBV minus a 20-bar SMA of OBV, from features.py) exceeds the threshold; if not, it falls back to a manual slope-comparison approach that normalises both price and OBV to [0,1] and computes linear-regression slopes via polyfit — slope_OBV minus slope_price becomes the divergence scalar.

Two signal branches exist: bullish (obv_div > threshold, signalling accumulation — long) and bearish (obv_div < -threshold, signalling distribution — short). The strategy DOES emit short signals via `side="short"`. The short branch uses `rsi_confirm` with the OVERSOLD condition as a gate, which is logically inverted (it blocks shorts when RSI is already oversold, i.e. the most obvious short opportunity), hinting at a logic error in the original code.

RSI confirmation for longs blocks entries when RSI > rsi_overbought (65), a sensible filter. ADX_max=40 is declared in PARAMS but never referenced in the strategy body — it is dead code. The strategy returns None immediately if already in a position, so it is always long-or-flat or short-or-flat, never pyramiding.

The `obv_divergence` feature in features.py is actually OBV minus OBV_SMA(20) — this is NOT a classic price/OBV divergence in the technical analysis sense; it is simply an OBV momentum oscillator. The manual fallback does implement a genuine price/OBV slope comparison but uses the same scalar for entry — mixing two semantically different quantities behind the same variable name creates inconsistent signal logic depending on whether the precomputed feature is available.

The strategy was pruned in Round 1 of the registry evolution (0% positive fitness across 29K evaluations), indicating it produced no net positive returns in the in-sample search across all param combinations tried. This is the most important fact for classification. The primary weaknesses are: (1) the `obv_divergence` precomputed feature is not a real divergence, making the fast path semantically wrong; (2) dead ADX filter; (3) inverted RSI logic on the short branch; (4) OBV is a cumulative absolute-volume series that is highly non-stationary and regime-dependent, making threshold comparisons fragile across years of data.
long_only_hypothesis: In spot long-only mode, the strategy becomes: buy when OBV is rising faster than price (accumulation phase signals smart-money buying before price catches up), hold with ATR-based stop and take-profit, and go to cash otherwise. The genuine edge hypothesis is that large buyers accumulate at declining or sideways prices, leaving a trail in OBV before price reacts. If this is real, the bullish divergence leg alone should generate positive expectancy on long crypto positions, particularly during the early phases of bull-market cycles. The strategy should be most effective in range-bound-to-breakout transitions, not during momentum-driven parabolic moves. To have any chance of passing OOS, the manual slope-comparison branch (which measures true price/OBV divergence) must be used as the primary signal rather than the OBV-minus-SMA proxy, and the RSI oversold confirmation should be added on the LONG branch (buy when OBV accelerating AND price weak/oversold, suggesting dip accumulation). The bearish branch is simply dropped — treat it as cash.
market_techniques: OBV slope divergence (linear regression slope difference on normalised price vs OBV over rolling window)
RSI oversold filter on longs (buy accumulation only when price is depressed, RSI < 40-50)
ATR-based stop loss and take profit for position management
ADX low filter to avoid pure trend-following situations where OBV and price move together (divergence is less meaningful in strong trends)
Volume surge confirmation (volume_ratio or volume_zscore spike at or just before entry confirms smart-money footprint)
BMSB (Bull Market Support Band) as macro regime gate — only take long signals when price is above BMSB, filtering bear market accumulation traps
Walk-forward expanding-window calibration of the divergence threshold using pre-lockbox history only
Bollinger Band width percentile as volatility-regime filter — prefer accumulation signals when BB is compressed (pre-breakout), avoid when bandwidth is wide (already in trend)
Time stop to limit exposure when divergence does not resolve into price movement
useful_features: volume: obv — cumulative on-balance volume (primary input)
volume: obv_divergence — OBV minus OBV_SMA(20), use as secondary momentum filter not primary signal
volume: volume_ratio — volume/SMA20-volume, confirm accumulation with above-average volume
volume: volume_zscore — standardised volume spike detector, useful to confirm breakout after accumulation
momentum: rsi_14 — RSI filter to confirm oversold condition on longs (avoid buying overbought OBV divergence)
momentum: rsi_7 — faster RSI for tighter timing of oversold entry
trend: adx_14 — low ADX (< 25) preferred for divergence signals; high ADX means price is already trending and OBV alignment is expected
volatility: atr_14 — stop and take-profit sizing
volatility: bb_bw_percentile — low percentile (< 30) indicates compression before breakout, ideal accumulation window
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass vol for vol-targeting position size
price: dist_from_low_20 — confirm price is near recent lows when buying bullish divergence (dip-accumulation context)
price: bb_pct_b — pct_b < 0.2 (price near lower band) strengthens bullish divergence entry case
regime: bmsb_bullish — macro bull regime gate, only go long when price > both BMSB bands
microstructure: buying_pressure / pressure_imbalance — bar-level buying pressure confirms accumulation footprint
microstructure: close_location — high close location (close near bar high) on divergence bar strengthens signal

genome_params.name: lookback
genome_params.note: Window for slope-comparison divergence; shorter is noisier, longer misses early entries

genome_params.name: obv_divergence_threshold
genome_params.note: Minimum slope difference to trigger; critical param — too low = noise, too high = rare signals

genome_params.name: rsi_oversold
genome_params.note: RSI upper bound for confirming price weakness on longs (buy accumulation when RSI is depressed)

genome_params.name: adx_max
genome_params.note: Maximum ADX to allow entry — divergence is more meaningful in low-trend environments

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for stop loss; 1.5-2.5 is the practical range for crypto hourly

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for take profit; reward/risk ratio dial

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Max hours to hold if divergence does not resolve into price move

genome_params.name: volume_ratio_min
genome_params.note: Minimum volume_ratio to confirm accumulation footprint at entry bar

genome_params.name: require_bmsb_bull
genome_params.note: Gate entries on bmsb_bullish macro regime flag

do_not_optimize: min_price_move_pct — currently dead code, no reference in logic body; fixing to a constant avoids a free degree of freedom that adds no value
rsi_overbought — for a long-only strategy there is no short branch; this param has no effect and should be removed from the genome entirely
require_regime / allowed_regimes — the regime classifier is not well-calibrated enough to be a genome parameter; fix require_regime=False or remove to avoid fitting to regime label noise
rsi_confirm (bool toggle) — always enable RSI confirmation on longs (fix to True) rather than allowing the genome to discover that removing a sanity check improves in-sample metrics

risk_management.stop: ATR-based: stop_loss = entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14. Minimum floor at 1.0x ATR. Checked against bar low each tick.
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-based: take_profit = entry_price + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14. Reward/risk ratio must be >= 1.5 enforced at signal-generation time; reject signals where ATR-implied R:R < 1.5.
risk_management.trailing: Optional trailing stop: once unrealised PnL > 1x ATR, move stop to breakeven; once > 2x ATR, trail at 1x ATR below running high. Helps lock in gains during genuine post-accumulation breakouts.
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-targeted sizing using gk_vol or atr_14/price as daily vol proxy. Target annualised vol of 30-40% of equity; cap position at max_position_pct. Use backtester sizing_method=volatility_scaled.
risk_management.max_size: Maximum 20% of equity per trade (max_position_pct=0.20). Reduce to 10% in high-volatility regimes (gk_vol > 90th percentile of trailing 90-day window).
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 6-hour cooldown between trades (min_bars_between_trades ~ 6 on hourly data) to avoid rapid-fire re-entry after stop-outs. Enforced via RiskManager min_interval.
risk_management.risk_off: Hard gate: do not enter new longs if bmsb_bullish is False AND adx_14 < 15 (choppy bear). Secondary gate: if strategy has suffered 3 consecutive stop-outs, pause for 48 hours (circuit-breaker mode). Daily loss limit: halt if equity drops > 5% from day-open.

metrics: CAGR (annualised compound return) — primary return metric; target > 20% for long-only crypto to justify the strategy vs buy-and-hold
Sharpe ratio (annualised, risk-free=0) — target > 0.8 OOS; was 0% positive fitness in Round 1 so any positive Sharpe in OOS is meaningful progress
Sortino ratio — more appropriate than Sharpe for long-only (downside deviation only); target > 1.0
Maximum drawdown % — hard cap at 30% for spot long-only; crypto buy-and-hold DD is 80%+, so beating that bar is easy but Calmar must still be positive
Calmar ratio (CAGR / maxDD) — target > 0.5; key quality signal for a strategy that can be in cash
Hit rate (% winning trades) — divergence strategies typically have <50% win rates with high R:R; monitor that R:R compensates
Turnover (average number of round-trips per month) — OBV divergence should generate 4-12 trades/month on hourly data; verify cost sensitivity
Gross/net exposure % (fraction of time in market) — target 20-50%; lower exposure (more time in cash) reduces drawdown but must be justified by signal quality
Average trade duration vs time_stop_hours — if most exits are via time_stop, the divergence is not resolving; reduce lookback or tighten threshold
Cost sensitivity (Sharpe vs commission_bps sweep from 2bp to 20bp) — a fragile strategy dies at 10bp; target < 20% Sharpe degradation from 5bp to 15bp
Bootstrap/Monte Carlo Sharpe confidence interval (1000 resamples of trade returns) — report 5th/95th percentile Sharpe; strategy must have CI lower bound > 0.3 to be credible
Per-year Sharpe stability (compute Sharpe on each calendar year of pre-lockbox data) — flag if any year is < -0.5 (fragile to regime)
OOS degradation % (walk-forward train vs test Sharpe drop) — target < 40% degradation; Round 1 result of 0% positive fitness suggests this will be very high
sweep_changes: DROP the short branch entirely: replace the bearish elif block with a return None so the strategy is strictly long-or-cash. Remove rsi_overbought from PARAMS and genome.

FIX RSI confirmation logic for longs: change the bullish gate from `rsi > rsi_overbought` (blocks buying when not overbought) to `rsi > rsi_oversold` (require price weakness), and make rsi_confirm always True for the long branch.

REMOVE the fast-path using the precomputed obv_divergence feature (OBV - OBV_SMA, which is an OBV momentum oscillator, not a price/OBV divergence). Always use the manual slope-comparison branch to ensure semantically correct divergence measurement. Alternatively, compute a correct precomputed feature (polyfit slopes of normalised price vs OBV) in features.py.
ADD adx_max enforcement: the PARAMS dict already declares adx_max=40 but it is never used in the code body. Add a guard: `if current.get('adx_14', 0) > p['adx_max']: return None`.

ADD volume_ratio confirmation: add a check that volume_ratio >= volume_ratio_min at the signal bar to confirm the accumulation is volume-backed. Add volume_ratio_min to PARAMS (default 1.0) and the genome param_space.
ADD bmsb_bullish macro gate: add optional require_bmsb_bull param; if True, skip longs when bmsb_bullish is False. Add to genome as bool param.
ADD bb_pct_b context filter: optionally require bb_pct_b < 0.4 (price in lower half of Bollinger Band) to confirm price weakness at accumulation entry.
ADD dist_from_low_20 filter: only enter long if dist_from_low_20 > -0.15 (price within 15% of 20-bar low) to focus on dip-accumulation context.

ADD to STRATEGY_REGISTRY in auto_evolve.py: re-add obv_divergence with the cleaned param_space covering lookback (int 10-40), obv_divergence_threshold (float 0.1-0.8), rsi_oversold (float 25-50), adx_max (float 20-45), stop_loss_atr_mult (float 1.0-4.0), take_profit_atr_mult (float 1.5-6.0), time_stop_hours (int 12-96), volume_ratio_min (float 0.8-2.0), require_bmsb_bull (bool).
VALIDATE that the manual polyfit branch handles edge cases: p_range==0 or o_range==0 already returns None (good), but also guard len(window) < lookback+1 which can occur near data boundaries.
methodological_risks: OBV non-stationarity: OBV is a cumulative series whose absolute level has no meaning across time; normalising to [0,1] per window and computing slopes partially addresses this but the slope differences are still sensitive to the volume regime (low-vol periods produce smaller slopes). The threshold 0.3 calibrated on 2019-2023 data may not generalise to 2024-2026 volume structures.

Feature mismatch — fast path vs slow path: the precomputed obv_divergence (OBV - OBV_SMA) and the manual slope-difference are not the same quantity. When the precomputed feature is available (which it always will be after features.py runs), the fast path always fires — meaning the manual slope-comparison is dead code in practice. This silent misuse produces a completely different signal than intended.

Round 1 fitness failure: 0% positive fitness across 29K evaluations is a strong negative prior. The signal may have no edge at any param setting, not just at the defaults. Re-adding it to the registry should only happen if the code fix (short removal + RSI logic fix + correct OBV divergence computation) is substantial enough to constitute a genuinely different strategy.

Lookback window data leakage risk: the manual slope comparison slices `df.iloc[bar_idx - lookback: bar_idx + 1]`, which is correct and does not leak. However if lookback is evolved to large values (e.g. 40 bars), early bars in a walk-forward fold may have insufficient warmup data, causing NaN-heavy periods that bias fitness.

RSI confirmation filter reduces trade count drastically: with rsi_oversold = 35 (default) and lookback=20 on hourly crypto, valid signals may be extremely rare (perhaps 1-3 per month), making walk-forward fold statistics unreliable (few trades per fold, high Sharpe variance).

Threshold overfitting: obv_divergence_threshold is a continuous param swept over [0.1, 0.8]. The optimal threshold is highly data-period dependent (in trending markets divergences rarely reach 0.5; in choppy markets many false divergences appear at 0.1). This is the primary overfitting axis — any optimised threshold on in-sample data is unlikely to transfer.

Time-stop interaction with divergence resolution: if the divergence does not resolve within time_stop_hours, the position exits at cost. In choppy regimes, most trades will hit time stops, creating a negative-expectancy drip from spread + commission without a winning tail. Monitor exit-reason distribution carefully.

Signal sparsity in the OOS lockbox (2024-2026): with tighter RSI filters and volume confirmation added, the strategy may generate fewer than 20 trades in the 2-year OOS window, making statistical conclusions about OOS performance unreliable.
